Name: Disable Auto-Shrink on Databases to Prevent Fragmentation and Performance Degradation
Description: The auto-shrink feature in SQL Server databases is a widely recognized anti-pattern and should be disabled on all production systems. When enabled, it forces the database engine to repeatedly shrink data files by releasing unused space back to the file system.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET AUTO_SHRINK OFF for every user database. Disable any maintenance plan shrink tasks. Verify with SELECT name, is_auto_shrink_on FROM sys.databases.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Disable Auto-Shrink on Databases to Prevent Fragmentation and Performance Degradation' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Auto-shrink is the most common self-inflicted performance wound in SQL Server. It fragments indexes globally in exchange for temporary disk space.

Name: Disable auto-close on all databases
Description: The AUTO_CLOSE database option, when enabled, automatically shuts down a database after the last connection is terminated. While this feature might be useful for isolated or temporary desktop databases, it severely degrades performance in production environments and high-traffic applications.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET AUTO_CLOSE OFF for every database. This is a one-line change with zero app impact and immediate latency elimination.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Disable auto-close on all databases' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Auto-close forces a cold database start for every new connection. Users experience multi-second delays where milliseconds are expected.

Name: Set PAGE_VERIFY = CHECKSUM on all databases
Description: Checks PAGE_VERIFY is set to CHECKSUM on every user database. CHECKSUM catches silent page corruption from failing IO subsystems. TORN_PAGE_DETECTION misses 50% of corruptions CHECKSUM catches.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET PAGE_VERIFY CHECKSUM for every database. Then run DBCC CHECKDB to establish a clean checksum baseline.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Set PAGE_VERIFY = CHECKSUM on all databases' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Optimize TempDB File Count to Prevent Allocation Contention
Description: The optimal configuration of TempDB data files within SQL Server. TempDB is used for temporary objects, such as sort operations and temp tables, which are critical components of many queries.
RecommendedAction: Add TempDB data files to match CPU core count up to 8. All files must be equal size and identical autogrowth. Restart SQL Server after the change.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Optimize TempDB File Count to Prevent Allocation Contention' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Set tempdb autogrowth to fixed MB (e.g., 512MB or 1GB), not percentage
Description: The autogrowth settings for the system TempDB database use fixed Megabyte (MB) increments rather than percentage-based growth. Using fixed MB values makes sure predictable Input/Output (I/O) patterns, preventing sudden and unpredictable file expansion which can lead to performance degradation, resource contention, and potential.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE tempdb MODIFY FILE (NAME='tempdev', FILEGROWTH=512MB) and repeat for each TempDB data file. Set log file to 256MB fixed growth.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Set tempdb autogrowth to fixed MB (e.g., 512MB or 1GB), not percentage' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: TempDB contention is the #1 cause of intermittent application timeouts. Fixing this removes a recurring source of user-facing slowness.

Name: Enable Trace Flag 174 to increase plan cache bucket count and reduce SOS_CACHESTORE contention
Description: The configuration of Trace Flag 174, which increases the number of plan cache buckets in SQL Server. When the default plan cache limit is reached on high-performance systems, low-cost query plans can be evicted to make room for new ones, leading to.
RecommendedAction: Enable trace flag 174 as a startup parameter: -T174. This increases plan cache hash buckets. Monitor SOS_CACHESTORE spinlock contention after enabling.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Enable Trace Flag 174 to increase plan cache bucket count and reduce SOS_CACHESTORE contention' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to NIST CP-2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

RecommendedAction: Apply SQL Server 2016 SP1 CU2 or later to fix KB3178297. If patching is not possible, manually trigger query store cleanup periodically.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Monitor Query Store Cleanup Health (KB3178297) â€” Prevent Disk Exhaustion' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Ensure Fixed Paging File Size is Set for Predictable Performance
Description: Verifies a fixed paging file size instead of system-managed. System-managed paging files resize during IO spikes, adding latency when the server is already under load.

Name: Configure Windows Power Plan to High Performance for SQL Server Availability
Description: Confirms the Windows Power Plan is High Performance. Balanced plan throttles CPU frequency and adds 10-50ms of latency to every CPU-bound query. invisible in perfmon, devastating to OLTP.
RecommendedAction: Set Windows Power Plan to 'High Performance' via Control Panel > Power Options. On VMs, also disable C-states in BIOS. Verify with powercfg /list.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Configure Windows Power Plan to High Performance for SQL Server Availability' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Verify Applied State of Critical SQL Server Configurations
Description: Critical SQL Server configuration options, which may have been modified using sp_configure, are fully applied and reflect the intended state. Configuration drift occurs when a setting is changed but not properly activated (e.g., forgetting to run RECONFIGURE).
RecommendedAction: Run RECONFIGURE immediately. Check for pending changes: SELECT name, value, value_in_use FROM sys.configurations WHERE value <> value_in_use.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Verify Applied State of Critical SQL Server Configurations' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Restrict or Disable the Default sa Login Account
Description: Verifies the sa account is disabled and renamed. Default-named sa is the #1 brute-force target in every breach report and every audit framework requires it to be locked down.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER LOGIN sa DISABLE. Then rename it: ALTER LOGIN sa WITH NAME='sql_sa_disabled_do_not_use'. Verify with SELECT name, is_disabled FROM sys.server_principals WHERE principal_id=1.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Restrict or Disable the Default sa Login Account' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Required by PCI-DSS, CIS, NIST, and every security framework. An enabled sa account is an automatic audit finding with a 30-day remediation deadline.

Name: Detect special characters (spaces, brackets, dashes, semicolons, quotes)
Description: Database objects (tables, views, stored procedures, etc.) adhere to strict naming conventions by avoiding special characters such as spaces, brackets, dashes, dots, commas, and semicolons. Using non-alphanumeric identifiers forces the use of delimiters (e.g., `[]`) in T-SQL code, which significantly increases code complexity, reduces readability, and introduces potential syntax error.
RecommendedAction: Rename objects to alphanumeric identifiers with underscores only. Use sp_rename. Update all referencing code and applications.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect special characters (spaces, brackets, dashes, semicolons, quotes)' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

RecommendedAction: column(s) contain non-standard characters. Use alphanumeric identifiers.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect non-standard characters in database object names (columns/tables)' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Detect tables with zero indexes â€” pure heaps that severely degrade performance
Description: Identifies heap tables with no clustered index. Heaps force full table scans on every data retrieval. A 100M-row heap table reads 100M rows to find one row. every single time.
RecommendedAction: Create a clustered index on every heap table. Start with the most-used column from WHERE clauses. Use sp_BlitzIndex to identify heap tables.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect tables with zero indexes â€” pure heaps that severely degrade performance' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 11.1, NIST CP-2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Detect duplicate indexes (same key columns) â€” waste storage, memory, and write performance
Description: Identifies indexes with duplicate key columns. Every INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE must maintain all duplicates. this turns single-write operations into multi-write IO storms.
RecommendedAction: Use sp_BlitzIndex @Mode=2 to find duplicate indexes. Drop the redundant index. Keep the one with the most includes or widest key for better coverage.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect duplicate indexes (same key columns) â€” waste storage, memory, and write performance' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.8, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Ensure Clustered Indexes are Unique for Optimal Performance
Description: Clustered indexes are defined on unique columns. When a clustered index is built on non-unique columns, SQL Server automatically appends a 4-byte integer uniqueifier to every duplicate key value.
RecommendedAction: Make clustered indexes unique by adding the primary key column to the key if needed. Non-unique clustered indexes add a 4-byte uniquifier to every row.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Ensure Clustered Indexes are Unique for Optimal Performance' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to NIST CP-2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: 19 Use Windows Authentication over SQL Authentication
Description: Checks that the server runs in Windows Authentication mode and counts active SQL logins. SQL logins bypass AD password policies for complexity, lockout, and rotation. auditors flag every one.
RecommendedAction: Switch to Windows Authentication mode: go to Server Properties > Security > Windows Authentication mode. Restart SQL Server. Or set via registry and restart.

RecommendedAction: Run sp_configure 'max server memory (MB)', <value> where value = total_ram_mb - max(4096, total_ram_mb * 0.10). Then RECONFIGURE. Verify with sys.dm_os_sys_memory.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Set max server memory to 85% of total RAM (min 0%) to prevent OS resource exhaustion' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Detect rarely used or redundant indexes to reduce write overhead and improve performance
Description: Identifies indexes with negligible read activity. Rarely used indexes cost more in write maintenance than they save in read performance. Monitor through 2 full business cycles before dropping.
RecommendedAction: Review rarely used indexes with sp_BlitzIndex @Mode=2. Drop indexes with zero reads and significant writes. Monitor through 2 full business cycles before dropping.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect rarely used or redundant indexes to reduce write overhead and improve performance' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Detect Untrusted Constraints (WITH NOCHECK) and Performance Risks
Description: Identifies FK and CHECK constraints marked as not trusted. An untrusted constraint is invisible to the query optimizer for cardinality estimation. plans go wrong silently.
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER TABLE [name] WITH CHECK CHECK CONSTRAINT [name] for each untrusted constraint. This revalidates data and makes the constraint trusted again for the optimizer.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect Untrusted Constraints (WITH NOCHECK) and Performance Risks' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Description: The PARAMETRIZATION database option controls how SQL Server handles literal values in queries. When set to FORCED, all queries are treated as parameterized, which can improve plan reuse and reduce compilation overhead but risks suboptimal plans due to.
RecommendedAction: Set PARAMETRIZATION to SIMPLE unless you have measured and documented the need for FORCED. Use ALTER DATABASE [name] SET PARAMETERIZATION SIMPLE.

Name: Detect user-defined functions using reserved prefixes (e.g., fn_) to prevent naming conflicts with system functions
Description: User-defined functions (UDFs) do not use reserved prefixes, such as 'fn_', which are designated for system functions in SQL Server. Using reserved or system-like prefixes can lead to naming conflicts, ambiguity, and potential instability if Microsoft modifies or deprecates the underlying system function names.

Description: Confirms SQL logins use CHECK_POLICY and CHECK_EXPIRATION. Weak SQL login passwords are the second most common entry point after sa attacks.

Description: For the unauthorized or unnecessary execution of the DBCC FREEPROCCACHE command. This command clears the entire procedure (plan) cache, forcing SQL Server to recompile all cached queries and plans.

Description: The database is configured to maintain auto-created column statistics incrementally on partitioned tables. When this setting is enabled, updating statistics does not require scanning the entire table, which significantly reduces I/O overhead and resource consumption.

These procedures run with elevated permissions, often before full authentication and authorization contexts are established.

RecommendedAction: Disable unnecessary logon triggers with DISABLE TRIGGER [name] ON ALL SERVER. Document each remaining trigger's purpose and owner.

RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET DATE_CORRELATION_OPTIMIZATION OFF. This feature has questionable benefit and adds maintenance overhead.

Description: Two critical SQL Server configuration areas. First, it identifies user-created tables within the MSDB system database that are not automatically included in standard backup routines, posing a significant risk of data.

Description: The Model Database acts as a template for all newly created databases on an instance. If unauthorized or non-standard user-created objects (like custom stored procedures, tables, or functions) reside in this model database, they are automatically copied to every new.

Description: DML triggers execute automatically upon INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE operations on user tables. While they are useful for enforcing complex business logic, unmanaged or poorly written triggers introduce significant hidden complexity and performance overhead.

Description: A database's transaction log files are physically separated from data files and other logs. SQL Server writes to the transaction log sequentially, meaning placing multiple log files on the same physical drive does not provide I/O parallelism benefits and.

Name: Verification of Complete and Current Database Backup Chains
Description: A complete, current, and secure backup chain exists for critical databases. Regular full backups are foundational to data recovery, making sure the Recovery Point Objective (RPO) is met by minimizing data loss.
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for Verification of Complete and Current Database Backup Chains. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Verification of Complete and Current Database Backup Chains' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.

Name: Configure Windows Power Plan to High Performance for Stable SQL Server Operation
Description: The underlying operating system power plan is set to "High Performance" (or equivalent) on the SQL Server host machine. The default "Balanced" or energy-saving plans can dynamically throttle CPU resources and adjust performance based on perceived load, which introduces unpredictable latency spikes for database.
SqlQuery:
DECLARE @ver INT; SET @ver=CONVERT(INT,SERVERPROPERTY('ProductMajorVersion')); IF @ver<10 BEGIN SELECT 'SKIP' AS result,CONCAT('Requires 2008+. Current: ',@ver) AS message; RETURN; END;
SELECT 'INFO' AS result,'Windows Power Plan cannot be validated from T-SQL. Verify in Control Panel > Power Options that High Performance plan is selected.' AS message;
RecommendedAction: Set Windows Power Plan to High Performance via powercfg.cpl. This is a 30-second change with measurable impact on CPU-bound queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Configure Windows Power Plan to High Performance for Stable SQL Server Operation' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: CPU throttling from Balanced power plan adds unpredictable latency to every query. This is a 30-second fix with measurable performance gain.

Name: Detect use of GUID (uniqueidentifier) in clustered index key â€“ causes severe fragmentation
Description: Using GUIDs (uniqueidentifier) as clustered index keys is highly discouraged because their random nature leads to frequent page splits and severe fragmentation. This process significantly degrades database performance over time, requiring costly maintenance operations.
RecommendedAction: Do not use GUIDs in clustered index keys. If you must, use NEWSEQUENTIALID() instead of NEWID(). Consider an integer identity column as the clustered key.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Detect use of GUID (uniqueidentifier) in clustered index key â€“ causes severe fragmentation' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to NIST CP-2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

RecommendedAction: Either drop disabled indexes if obsolete, or rebuild them if needed. Disabled indexes still consume storage and block index maintenance.

RecommendedAction: Remove shrink tasks from all maintenance plans. Replace with index reorganize/rebuild. Shrink destroys index fragmentation in exchange for temporary disk space.

Name: Pre-size database files and configure fixed auto-growth increments for stability

RecommendedAction: Grow the transaction log in 8 GB increments to a target size. Then set autogrowth to 512 MB fixed. Check VLF count with DBCC LOGINFO.

BusinessTranslation: Without log backups, point-in-time recovery is impossible. A data-loss event with no recent backups is a resume-generating incident.

when executing stored procedures or complex queries involving temporary tables (which

RecommendedAction: Fix collation mismatch: rebuild the database with the correct collation or use COLLATE in cross-database queries. Mismatched collations cause sort errors and tempdb spills.

SqlQuery:
DECLARE @ver INT; SET @ver=CONVERT(INT,SERVERPROPERTY('ProductMajorVersion')); IF @ver<10 BEGIN SELECT 'SKIP' AS result,CONCAT('Requires 2008+. Current: ',@ver) AS message; RETURN; END;
SELECT 'INFO' AS result, 'SMBv1 status cannot be checked from T-SQL. Verify via Get-SmbServerConfiguration | Select EnableSMB1Protocol in PowerShell.' AS message;
RecommendedAction: Disable SMBv1 via Windows Features or PowerShell: Disable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName SMB1Protocol. SMBv1 is deprecated and a known attack vector.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Disable Legacy Protocols (SMBv1) to Reduce Attack Surface' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Backups happening in the last 7 days
Description: Verifies full backups completed within 7 days. A server with no recent backups is one disk failure away from total data loss. This is the single most basic check and the one that burns people most often.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
               SELECT 1 AS CheckID ,
  d.[name] AS DatabaseName ,
  1 AS Priority ,
  'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Backups Not Performed Recently' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nobak' AS URL ,
  'Last backed up: '
  + COALESCE(CAST(MAX(b.backup_finish_date) AS VARCHAR(25)),'never') AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases d
  LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset b ON d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
     AND b.type = 'D'
     AND b.server_name = SERVERPROPERTY('ServerName') /*Backupset ran on current server */
WHERE d.database_id <> 2 /* Bonus points if you know what that means */
  AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
  AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
  AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
  -- AND d.name NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT
     -- DatabaseName
    --FROM #SkipChecks
  --  WHERE CheckID IS NULL OR CheckID = 1)
  /*
  The above NOT IN filters out the databases we're not supposed to check.
  */
GROUP BY d.name
HAVING MAX(b.backup_finish_date) <= DATEADD(dd,
     -7, GETDATE())
  OR MAX(b.backup_finish_date) IS NULL
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Schedule full backups for every database. Verify with: SELECT database_name, MAX(backup_finish_date) FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type='D' GROUP BY database_name.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Backups happening in the last 7 days' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.

Name: Transaction Log Backup taking place
Description: Checks log backups are running on schedule. Without log backups, the transaction log grows until the drive fills, the database goes read-only, and production stops cold.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT DISTINCT
 2 AS CheckID ,
 d.name AS DatabaseName ,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Full Recovery Model w/o Log Backups' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/biglogs' AS URL ,
 ( 'The ' + CAST(CAST((SELECT ((SUM([mf].[size]) * 8.) / 1024.) FROM sys.[master_files] AS [mf] WHERE [mf].[database_id] = d.[database_id] AND [mf].[type_desc] = 'LOG') AS DECIMAL(18,2)) AS VARCHAR(30)) + 'MB log file has not been backed up in the last week.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases d
--LEFT JOIN #DBCCs ll On ll.DbName = d.name And ll.Field = 'dbi_LastLogBackupTime'
WHERE d.recovery_model IN ( 1, 2 )
 AND d.database_id NOT IN ( 2, 3 )
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL
 AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
 AND (
 (
 /* We couldn't get a value from the DBCC DBINFO data so let's check the msdb backup history information */
 --[ll].[Value] Is Null
 --AND 
 NOT EXISTS ( SELECT *
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset b
  WHERE d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
   AND b.type = 'L'
   AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,-7, GETDATE())
 )
 )
 
)
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Schedule log backups every 5-15 minutes for databases in FULL recovery model. Check with: SELECT database_name, MAX(backup_finish_date) FROM msdb.dbo.backupset WHERE type='L' GROUP BY database_name.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Transaction Log Backup taking place' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Without log backups, point-in-time recovery is impossible. A data-loss event with no recent backups is a resume-generating incident.

Name: MSDB Backup History Not Purged
Description: Checks msdb backup history tables have been cleaned up recently. Unchecked, these tables grow to 50+ GB and slow every backup-related operation, including restore planning.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT TOP 1
 3 AS CheckID ,
 'msdb' ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'MSDB Backup History Not Purged' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/history' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database backup history retained back to '
  + CAST(bs.backup_start_date AS VARCHAR(20)) ) AS Details
 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
 LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON bs.database_name = rh.destination_database_name
 WHERE rh.destination_database_name IS NULL
 ORDER BY bs.backup_start_date ASC

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Run sp_delete_backuphistory with an appropriate retention date. Or use Ola Hallengren's DatabaseBackup with @CleanupTime.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'MSDB Backup History Not Purged' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 11.1, NIST CP-9. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Sysadmins on SQL server
Description: Lists every sysadmin on the instance. Every sysadmin can drop databases, read all data, and disable auditing. This list should be short, documented, and reviewed monthly.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 4 AS CheckID ,
 230 AS Priority ,
 'Security' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Sysadmins' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/sa' AS URL ,
 ( 'Login [' + l.name
 + '] is a sysadmin - meaning they can do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.syslogins l
WHERE l.sysadmin = 1
 AND l.name <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01)
 AND l.denylogin = 0
 AND l.name NOT LIKE 'NT SERVICE\%'
 AND l.name <> 'l_certSignSmDetach' /* Added in SQL 2016 */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Remove unnecessary sysadmins. Every sysadmin that is not a named DBA account is a risk. Run: EXEC sp_helpsrvrolemember 'sysadmin'.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Sysadmins on SQL server' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 5.2, NIST IA-2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

 + '] is a security admin - meaning they can give themselves permission to do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' ) AS Details
FROM master.sys.syslogins l
WHERE l.securityadmin = 1
 AND l.name <> SUSER_SNAME(0x01)

 ) THEN 1

END

Name: Database Auto-Close enabled
Description: Detects AUTO_CLOSE enabled on any database. AUTO_CLOSE shuts down the database after the last connection leaves, then forces a cold start for the next user. adding second-level latency to every.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 12 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 10 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Auto-Close Enabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/autoclose' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-close enabled. This setting can dramatically decrease performance.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_close_on = 1
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET AUTO_CLOSE OFF for every database that has it enabled.

BusinessTranslation: Auto-close forces a cold database start for every new connection. Users experience multi-second delays where milliseconds are expected.

Name: Database Auto-Shrink enabled

SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 13 AS CheckID ,
 [name] AS DatabaseName ,
 10 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Auto-Shrink Enabled' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/autoshrink' AS URL ,
 ( 'Database [' + [name]
 + '] has auto-shrink enabled. This setting can dramatically decrease performance.' ) AS Details
FROM sys.databases
WHERE is_auto_shrink_on = 1
 AND state <> 6 /* Offline */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Run ALTER DATABASE [name] SET AUTO_SHRINK OFF for every database. Disable any maintenance plan shrink tasks.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Database Auto-Shrink enabled' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Auto-shrink is the most common self-inflicted performance wound in SQL Server. It fragments indexes globally in exchange for temporary disk space.

Name: Corruption checks not optimal
Description: DBCC CHECKDB corruption checks not running optimally.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

 SELECT 14 AS CheckID,
 [name] as DatabaseName,
 50 AS Priority,
 'Reliability' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Page Verification Not Optimal' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/torn' AS URL,
 ('Database [' + [name] + '] has ' + [page_verify_option_desc] + ' for page verification. SQL Server may have a harder time recognizing and recovering from storage corruption. Consider using CHECKSUM instead.') COLLATE database_default AS Details
 FROM sys.databases
 WHERE page_verify_option < 2
 AND name <> 'tempdb'
 AND state NOT IN (1, 6) /* Restoring, Offline */
  
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Schedule weekly DBCC CHECKDB via Ola Hallengren's DatabaseIntegrityCheck. Review output for errors.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Corruption checks not optimal' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: Database Corruption Detected
Description: Database corruption detected by DBCC CHECKDB or suspect_pages.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT
    34 AS CheckID ,

    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/repair' AS URL ,

    FROM (SELECT rp2.database_id, rp2.modification_time
   FROM sys.dm_db_mirroring_auto_page_repair rp2
   WHERE rp2.[database_id] not in (
   SELECT db2.[database_id]
   FROM sys.databases as db2
   WHERE db2.[state] = 1
   ) ) as rp
    INNER JOIN master.sys.databases db ON rp.database_id = db.database_id
    WHERE   rp.modification_time >= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE())
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END

Name: Single-Use Plans in Procedure Cache
Description: High count of single-use plans in the procedure cache. plan cache bloat. Users feel this as slow page loads and timeout errors.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN

SELECT  35 AS CheckID ,
   100 AS Priority ,
   'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
   'Single-Use Plans in Procedure Cache' AS Finding ,
   'https://www.brentozar.com/go/single' AS URL ,
   ( CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(10))
     + ' query plans are taking up memory in the procedure cache. This may be wasted memory if we cache plans for queries that never get called again. This may be a good use case for SQL Server 2008''s Optimize for Ad Hoc or for Forced Parameterization.' ) AS Details
 FROM    sys.dm_exec_cached_plans AS cp
 WHERE   cp.usecounts = 1
   AND cp.objtype = 'Adhoc'
   AND EXISTS ( SELECT
         1
       FROM sys.configurations
       WHERE
         name = 'optimize for ad hoc workloads'
         AND value_in_use = 0 )
 HAVING  COUNT(*) > 1
) 
THEN 1

    END

Name: Max Memory Set Too High
Description: Max server memory set above available physical RAM. Users feel this as slow page loads and timeout errors.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  50 AS CheckID ,
    100 AS Priority ,
    'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
    'Max Memory Set Too High' AS Finding ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/max' AS URL ,
    'SQL Server max memory is set to '
   + CAST(c.value_in_use AS VARCHAR(20))
   + ' megabytes, but the server only has '
   + CAST(( CAST(m.total_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) / 1024 ) AS VARCHAR(20))
   + ' megabytes.  SQL Server may drain the system dry of memory, and under certain conditions, this can cause Windows to swap to disk.' AS Details
    FROM    sys.dm_os_sys_memory m
    INNER JOIN sys.configurations c ON c.name = 'max server memory (MB)'
    WHERE   CAST(m.total_physical_memory_kb AS BIGINT) < ( CAST(c.value_in_use AS BIGINT) * 1024 )
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Reduce max server memory. Formula: total RAM - max(4 GB, 10% of total RAM). Run: sp_configure 'max server memory (MB)', <value>; RECONFIGURE.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Max Memory Set Too High' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  62 AS CheckID ,
  [name] AS DatabaseName ,
  200 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Old Compatibility Level' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/compatlevel' AS URL ,
  ( 'Database ' + [name]
    + ' is compatibility level '
    + CAST(compatibility_level AS VARCHAR(20))
    + ', which may cause unwanted results when trying to run queries that have newer T-SQL features.' ) AS Details
FROM    sys.databases
WHERE   compatibility_level <= 90
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Tune the query or configuration related to Old Compatibility Level. Check execution plan and wait statistics.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Old Compatibility Level' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Availability Groups has automatically repaired corruption

WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT DISTINCT

    'Corruption' AS FindingsGroup ,

Name: Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside
Description: Backups writing to the same physical drive as database files.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT
 93 AS CheckID ,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/backup' AS URL ,
 CAST(COUNT(1) AS VARCHAR(50)) + ' backups done on drive '
 + UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3))
 + ' in the last two weeks, where database files also live. This represents a serious risk if that array fails.' Details
FROM msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
 INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset AS bs ON bmf.media_set_id = bs.media_set_id
   AND bs.backup_start_date >= ( DATEADD(dd,
   -14, GETDATE()) )
 /* Filter out databases that were recently restored: */
 LEFT OUTER JOIN msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh ON bs.database_name = rh.destination_database_name AND rh.restore_date > DATEADD(dd, -14, GETDATE())
WHERE UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3)) <> 'HTT' AND
  bmf.physical_device_name NOT LIKE '\\%' AND -- GitHub Issue #2141
  -- @IsWindowsOperatingSystem = 1 AND -- GitHub Issue #1995
  UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, 3)) IN (
 SELECT DISTINCT
 UPPER(LEFT(mf.physical_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS, 3))
 FROM sys.master_files AS mf
  WHERE mf.database_id <> 2 )
 AND rh.destination_database_name IS NULL
GROUP BY UPPER(LEFT(bmf.physical_device_name, 3))
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Backing Up to Same Drive Where Databases Reside' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 11.1, NIST CP-9. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails
Description: SQL Agent jobs without failure notification emails.
SqlQuery:
;WITH las_job_run AS (
           SELECT  MAX(instance_id) AS instance_id, 
             job_id, COUNT_BIG(*) AS job_executions,
             SUM(CASE WHEN run_status = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS failed_executions
           FROM msdb.dbo.sysjobhistory
           WHERE step_id = 0
           GROUP BY job_id
           )
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT  94 AS CheckID ,
  200 AS [Priority] ,
  'Monitoring' AS FindingsGroup ,
  'Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails' AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/alerts' AS URL ,
  'The job ' + [name]
  + ' has not been set up to notify an operator if it fails.' 
  + CASE 
   WHEN jh.run_date IS NULL OR jh.run_time IS NULL OR jh.run_status IS NULL THEN ''
   ELSE N' Executions: '+ CAST(ljr.job_executions AS VARCHAR(30)) 
    + CASE ljr.failed_executions
     WHEN 0 THEN N''
     ELSE N' ('+CAST(ljr.failed_executions AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' failed)'
     END
    + N' - last execution started on '
    + CAST(CONVERT(DATE,CAST(jh.run_date AS NVARCHAR(8)),113) AS NVARCHAR(10)) 
    + N', at ' 
    + STUFF(STUFF(RIGHT(N'000000' + CAST(jh.run_time AS varchar(6)),6),3,0,N':'),6,0,N':')
    + N', with status "'
    + CASE jh.run_status 
      WHEN 0 THEN N'Failed'
      WHEN 1 THEN N'Succeeded'
      WHEN 2 THEN N'Retry'
      WHEN 3 THEN N'Canceled'
      WHEN 4 THEN N'In Progress'
     END +N'".'
   END AS Details
FROM    msdb.[dbo].[sysjobs] j
  LEFT JOIN las_job_run ljr 
   ON ljr.job_id = j.job_id
  LEFT JOIN msdb.[dbo].[sysjobhistory] jh
   ON ljr.job_id = jh.job_id AND ljr.instance_id = jh.instance_id
WHERE   j.enabled = 1
  AND j.notify_email_operator_id = 0
  AND j.notify_netsend_operator_id = 0
  AND j.notify_page_operator_id = 0
          AND j.category_id <> 100
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Agent Jobs Without Failure Emails' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Login Can Control Server
Description: Login has CONTROL SERVER permission.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (
SELECT 104 AS [CheckID] ,
 230 AS [Priority] ,
 'Security' AS [FindingsGroup] ,
 'Login Can Control Server' AS [Finding] ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/sa' AS [URL] ,
 'Login [' + pri.[name]
 + '] has the CONTROL SERVER permission - meaning they can do absolutely anything in SQL Server, including dropping databases or hiding their tracks.' AS [Details]
FROM sys.server_principals AS pri
WHERE pri.[principal_id] IN (
 SELECT p.[grantee_principal_id]
 FROM sys.server_permissions AS p
 WHERE p.[state] IN ( 'G', 'W' )
 AND p.[class] = 100
 AND p.[type] = 'CL' )
 AND pri.[name] NOT LIKE '##%##'
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Revoke CONTROL SERVER from logins that do not require it: REVOKE CONTROL SERVER FROM [login]. This permission is sysadmin-equivalent for most operations.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Login Can Control Server' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 5.2, NIST IA-2, PCI-DSS 8.2. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Poison Wait Detected
Description: Poison wait detected. THREADPOOL, RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE, or similar.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  107 AS CheckID ,
  50 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Poison Wait Detected: ' + wait_type  AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/poison/#' + wait_type AS URL ,
  CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000) / 86400) + ':' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(20), DATEADD(s, (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000), 0), 108) + ' of this wait have been recorded. This wait often indicates killer performance problems.'
FROM sys.[dm_os_wait_stats]
WHERE wait_type IN('IO_QUEUE_LIMIT', 'IO_RETRY', 'LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'POOL_LOG_RATE_GOVERNOR', 'PREEMPTIVE_DEBUG', 'RESMGR_THROTTLED', 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE', 'RESOURCE_SEMAPHORE_QUERY_COMPILE','SE_REPL_CATCHUP_THROTTLE','SE_REPL_COMMIT_ACK','SE_REPL_COMMIT_TURN','SE_REPL_ROLLBACK_ACK','SE_REPL_SLOW_SECONDARY_THROTTLE','THREADPOOL')
GROUP BY wait_type
HAVING SUM([wait_time_ms]) > (SELECT 5000 * datediff(HH,create_date,CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS hours_since_startup FROM sys.databases WHERE name='tempdb')
AND SUM([wait_time_ms]) > 60000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for Poison Wait Detected. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Poison Wait Detected' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Possibly Broken Log Shipping
Description: Possibly broken log shipping configuration.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

  SELECT  111 AS CheckID ,
    50 AS Priority ,
    'Reliability' AS FindingGroup ,
    'Possibly Broken Log Shipping'  AS Finding ,
    d.[name] ,
    'https://www.brentozar.com/go/shipping' AS URL ,
    d.[name] + ' is in a restoring state, but has not had a backup applied in the last two days. This is a possible indication of a broken transaction log shipping setup.'
    FROM [master].sys.databases d
    INNER JOIN [master].sys.database_mirroring dm ON d.database_id = dm.database_id
     AND dm.mirroring_role IS NULL
    WHERE ( d.[state] = 1
    OR (d.[state] = 0 AND d.[is_in_standby] = 1) )
    AND NOT EXISTS(SELECT * FROM msdb.dbo.restorehistory rh
    INNER JOIN msdb.dbo.backupset bs ON rh.backup_set_id = bs.backup_set_id
    WHERE d.[name] COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = rh.destination_database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
    AND rh.restore_date >= DATEADD(dd, -2, GETDATE()))
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for Possibly Broken Log Shipping. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Possibly Broken Log Shipping' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 11.1, NIST CP-9. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently
Description: TDE certificate not backed up recently. Without verified backups, this instance is one disk failure from data loss.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 119 AS CheckID,
  1 AS Priority,
  'Backup' AS FindingsGroup,
  'TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' AS Finding,
  db_name(dek.database_id) AS DatabaseName,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/tde' AS URL,
  'The certificate ' + c.name + ' is used to encrypt database ' + db_name(dek.database_id) + '. Last backup date: ' + COALESCE(CAST(c.pvt_key_last_backup_date AS VARCHAR(100)), 'Never') AS Details
  FROM master.sys.certificates c INNER JOIN sys.dm_database_encryption_keys dek ON c.thumbprint = dek.encryptor_thumbprint
  WHERE pvt_key_last_backup_date IS NULL OR pvt_key_last_backup_date <= DATEADD(dd, -30, GETDATE()) 

 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'TDE Certificate Not Backed Up Recently' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Backup gaps mean unrecoverable data. The business impact of 24 hours of lost transactions typically exceeds $100K in most industries.

Name: Poison Wait Detected: Serializable Sleeping
Description: Poison wait: Serializable sleeping locks causing blocking chains.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT  121 AS CheckID ,
  50 AS Priority ,
  'Performance' AS FindingGroup ,
  'Poison Wait Detected: Serializable Locking'  AS Finding ,
  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/serializable' AS URL ,
  CONVERT(VARCHAR(10), (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000) / 86400) + ':' + CONVERT(VARCHAR(20), DATEADD(s, (SUM([wait_time_ms]) / 1000), 0), 108) + ' of LCK_M_R% waits have been recorded. This wait often indicates killer performance problems.'
FROM sys.[dm_os_wait_stats]
WHERE wait_type IN ('LCK_M_RS_S', 'LCK_M_RS_U', 'LCK_M_RIn_NL','LCK_M_RIn_S', 'LCK_M_RIn_U','LCK_M_RIn_X', 'LCK_M_RX_S', 'LCK_M_RX_U','LCK_M_RX_X')
HAVING SUM([wait_time_ms]) > (SELECT 5000 * datediff(HH,create_date,CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) AS hours_since_startup FROM sys.databases WHERE name='tempdb')
AND SUM([wait_time_ms]) > 60000
) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END
RecommendedAction: Identify the lead serializable blocker. Serializable isolation holds range locks that cascade into blocking chains. Switch to READ COMMITTED SNAPSHOT if application allows.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Check 121' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Mapped to CIS 4.1, NIST CM-6. Unremediated findings become auditor exceptions during compliance reviews.

Name: Many Plans for One Query
Description: Many plans for a single query. plan cache pollution.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sys.all_columns WHERE name = 'query_hash')
     AND EXISTS (
SELECT TOP 1 160 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
 'Many Plans for One Query' AS Finding,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/parameterization' AS URL,
 CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) AS NVARCHAR(50)) + ' plans are present for a single query in the plan cache - meaning we probably have parameterization issues.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_exec_query_stats qs
    CROSS APPLY sys.dm_exec_plan_attributes(qs.plan_handle) pa
    WHERE pa.attribute = 'dbid'
    GROUP BY qs.query_hash, pa.value
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) > (SELECT COUNT(*) * 2 FROM sys.databases)
 ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT plan_handle) DESC
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for Many Plans for One Query. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Many Plans for One Query' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.

Name: High Number of Cached Plans

SqlQuery:

SELECT TOP 1 161 AS CheckID,
 100 AS Priority,

 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/planlimits' AS URL,
 'Your server configuration is limited to ' + CAST(ht.buckets_count * 4 AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' ' + ht.name + ', and you are currently caching ' + CAST(cc.entries_count AS VARCHAR(20)) + '.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_memory_cache_hash_tables ht
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_memory_cache_counters cc ON ht.name = cc.name AND ht.type = cc.type
 where ht.name IN ( 'SQL Plans' , 'Object Plans' , 'Bound Trees' )
 AND cc.entries_count >= (3 * ht.buckets_count)
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Enable 'optimize for ad hoc workloads' and consider reducing max server memory to free space for plan cache. Review single-use plan count.

Name: Too Much Free Memory

SELECT 165, 50, 'Performance', 'Too Much Free Memory', 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/freememory',
  CAST((CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024 ) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB of free memory inside SQL Server''s buffer pool, which is ' + CAST((CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) / 1024 / 1024) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + N'GB. You would think lots of free memory would be good, but check out the URL for more information.' AS Details
 FROM sys.dm_os_performance_counters cFree
 INNER JOIN sys.dm_os_performance_counters cTotal ON cTotal.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cTotal.counter_name = N'Total Server Memory (KB)                                                                                                        '
 WHERE cFree.object_name LIKE N'%Memory Manager%'
  AND cFree.counter_name = N'Free Memory (KB)                                                                                                                '
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) > 20480000000
  AND CAST(cTotal.cntr_value AS BIGINT) * .3 <= CAST(cFree.cntr_value AS BIGINT)
        AND CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('edition') AS VARCHAR(100)) NOT LIKE '%Standard%'
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END

Name: Snapshot Backups Occurring
Description: SQL Server is performing volume snapshot backups (using VSS or third-party snapshot technology). While valid, snapshot backups require proper configuration and may affect SQL Server's log truncation behavior if not properly acknowledged.

SELECT CASE 
WHEN EXISTS (

SELECT 178 AS CheckID ,
 200 AS Priority ,
 'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Snapshot Backups Occurring' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/snaps' AS URL ,
 ( CAST(COUNT(*) AS VARCHAR(20)) + ' snapshot-looking backups have occurred in the last two weeks, indicating that IO may be freezing up.') AS Details
 FROM msdb.dbo.backupset bs
 WHERE bs.type = 'D'
 AND bs.backup_size >= 50000000000 /* At least 50GB */
 AND DATEDIFF(SECOND, bs.backup_start_date, bs.backup_finish_date) <= 60 /* Backup took less than 60 seconds */
 AND bs.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -14, GETDATE()) /* In the last 2 weeks */
 ) THEN 1
 ELSE 0
END

SELECT 198 AS CheckID,
                  NULL AS DatabaseName,
                  10 AS Priority,
                  'Performance' AS FindingsGroup,
                  'CPU w/Odd Number of Cores' AS Finding,
                  'https://www.brentozar.com/go/oddity' AS URL,

                    END AS Details
           FROM   sys.dm_os_schedulers
           WHERE  is_online = 1
                  AND scheduler_id < 255

                  is_online
           HAVING ( COUNT(cpu_id) + 2 ) % 2 = 1


  )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END

Name: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Usage Advisory
Description: DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ran recently. plan cache flushed.
SqlQuery:
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#fnTraceGettable') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #fnTraceGettable;
  CREATE TABLE #fnTraceGettable
   (
     TextData NVARCHAR(4000) ,
     DatabaseName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     EventClass INT ,
     Severity INT ,
     StartTime DATETIME ,
     EndTime DATETIME ,
     Duration BIGINT ,
     NTUserName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     NTDomainName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     HostName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     ApplicationName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     LoginName NVARCHAR(256) ,
     DBUserName NVARCHAR(256)
    );
IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#dbcc_events_from_trace') IS NOT NULL
   DROP TABLE #dbcc_events_from_trace;

SELECT UPPER(
     REPLACE(
      SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), 0,
        ISNULL(
         NULLIF(
          CHARINDEX('(', CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)),
           0),
           LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )) --This replaces everything up to an open paren, if one exists.
          , SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 )
        , '') --This replaces any optional WITH clause to a DBCC command, like tableresults.
     ) AS [dbcc_event_trunc_upper],
   UPPER(
    REPLACE(
     CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData), SUBSTRING(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData),
           ISNULL(
            NULLIF(
             CHARINDEX(' WITH ',CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData))
             , 0),
            LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1),
             LEN(CONVERT(NVARCHAR(MAX), t.TextData)) + 1 ), '')) AS [dbcc_event_full_upper],
   MIN(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS min_start_time,
   MAX(t.StartTime) OVER (PARTITION BY CONVERT(NVARCHAR(128), t.TextData)) AS max_start_time,
   t.NTUserName AS [nt_user_name],
   t.NTDomainName AS [nt_domain_name],
   t.HostName AS [host_name],
      t.ApplicationName AS [application_name],
   t.LoginName [login_name],
   t.DBUserName AS [db_user_name]
    INTO #dbcc_events_from_trace
      FROM #fnTraceGettable AS t
   WHERE t.EventClass = 116
 
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
SELECT 207 AS CheckID ,
             10 AS Priority ,
             'Performance' AS FindingsGroup ,
             'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS Ran Recently' AS Finding ,
             'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/dbcc' AS URL ,
             'The user ' + COALESCE(d.nt_user_name, d.login_name) + ' has run DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS ' + CAST(COUNT(*) AS NVARCHAR(100)) + ' times between ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30), MIN(d.min_start_time)) + ' and ' + CONVERT(NVARCHAR(30),  MAX(d.max_start_time)) +
       '. If this is a production box, know that you''re clearing all data out of memory when this happens. What kind of monster would do that?'
       AS Details
     FROM    #dbcc_events_from_trace d
     WHERE d.dbcc_event_full_upper = N'DBCC DROPCLEANBUFFERS'

Name: Cumulative Update Available

SqlQuery:
DECLARE @ProductVersionMajor DECIMAL(10,2) ,@ProductVersion NVARCHAR(128),@ProductVersionMinor DECIMAL(10,2)

SET @ProductVersion = CAST(SERVERPROPERTY('ProductVersion') AS NVARCHAR(128));
  SELECT @ProductVersionMajor = SUBSTRING(@ProductVersion, 1,CHARINDEX('.', @ProductVersion) + 1 )
,@ProductVersionMinor = PARSENAME(CONVERT(varchar(32), @ProductVersion), 2);

IF EXISTS (SELECT * FROM INFORMATION_SCHEMA.TABLES WHERE TABLE_NAME = 'SqlServerVersions' AND TABLE_TYPE = 'BASE TABLE')
BEGIN
     SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (
        SELECT TOP 1 217, 100, 'Reliability', 'Cumulative Update Available', COALESCE(v.Url, 'https://SQLServerUpdates.com/'),
      v.MinorVersionName + ' was released on ' + CAST(CONVERT(DATETIME, v.ReleaseDate, 112) AS VARCHAR(100))
         FROM dbo.SqlServerVersions v
         WHERE v.MajorVersionNumber = @ProductVersionMajor
           AND v.MinorVersionNumber > @ProductVersionMinor
           AND  SERVERPROPERTY('EngineEdition') <> 8 
         ORDER BY v.MinorVersionNumber DESC     
     )
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
END

Name: Log Backups to NUL
Description: Log backups writing to NUL. backups are being discarded. Without verified backups, this instance is one disk failure from data loss.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE 
       WHEN EXISTS (
       SELECT DISTINCT
 256 AS CheckID ,
 d.name AS DatabaseName,
 1 AS Priority ,
 'Backup' AS FindingsGroup ,
 'Log Backups to NUL' AS Finding ,
 'https://www.brentozar.com/go/nul' AS URL ,
 N'The transaction log file has been backed up ' + CAST((SELECT count(*)
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
   -7, GETDATE())) AS NVARCHAR(8)) + ' time(s) to ''NUL'' in the last week, which means the backup does not exist. This breaks point-in-time recovery.' AS Details
FROM master.sys.databases AS d
WHERE d.recovery_model IN ( 1, 2 )
 AND d.database_id NOT IN ( 2, 3 )
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL
 AND d.state NOT IN(1, 6, 10) /* Not currently offline or restoring, like log shipping databases */
 AND d.is_in_standby = 0 /* Not a log shipping target database */
 AND d.source_database_id IS NULL /* Excludes database snapshots */
 --AND d.name NOT IN ( SELECT DISTINCT
 --  DatabaseName
 --  FROM #SkipChecks
 --  WHERE CheckID IS NULL OR CheckID = 2)
 AND EXISTS ( SELECT *
  FROM msdb.dbo.backupset AS b INNER JOIN
  msdb.dbo.backupmediafamily AS bmf
   ON b.media_set_id = bmf.media_set_id
  WHERE d.name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS = b.database_name COLLATE SQL_Latin1_General_CP1_CI_AS
  AND bmf.physical_device_name = 'NUL'
  AND b.type = 'L'
  AND b.backup_finish_date >= DATEADD(dd,
     -7, GETDATE()) )
       ) THEN 1
       ELSE 0
      END
RecommendedAction: Schedule and verify backups for Log Backups to NUL. Run RESTORE VERIFYONLY monthly on every backup file.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'Log Backups to NUL' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Without log backups, point-in-time recovery is impossible. A data-loss event with no recent backups is a resume-generating incident.

     SELECT
  DatabaseName = 
                       d.name,

                       257,
                   Priority = 
                       50,
                   FindingsGroup = 
                       N'Performance',
                   Finding =

                   Details = 
                       N'The database ' +
                       QUOTENAME(d.name) +
                       N' has a target recovery interval of ' +
                       RTRIM(d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds) + 
                       CASE

               FROM sys.databases AS d

               AND   d.is_read_only = 0
               AND   d.is_in_standby = 0
               AND   d.target_recovery_time_in_seconds <> 60
    
  )
  THEN 1
  ELSE 0
 END

Name: AG Replica Falling Behind
Description: AG replica is falling behind the primary in log redo. A lagging secondary widens the data-loss window in a failover event.
SqlQuery:
SELECT CASE WHEN
     EXISTS (             
SELECT 268 AS CheckID,
              5 AS Priority,
           DB_NAME(ps.database_id),
              'Availability' AS FindingsGroup,
              'AG Replica Falling Behind' AS Finding,
              'https://www.BrentOzar.com/go/ag' AS URL,
              ag.name + N' AG replica server ' + 
          ar.replica_server_name + N' is ' + 
          CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(SECOND, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) < 200 THEN (CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, drs.last_commit_time, ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' seconds ')
          ELSE (CAST(DATEDIFF(MINUTE, ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) AS NVARCHAR(10)) + N' minutes ') END
          + N' behind the primary.'
          AS details
       FROM sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS drs
       JOIN sys.availability_replicas AS ar ON drs.replica_id = ar.replica_id
       JOIN sys.availability_groups AS ag ON ar.group_id = ag.group_id
       JOIN sys.dm_hadr_database_replica_states AS ps 
        ON drs.group_id = ps.group_id 
        AND drs.database_id = ps.database_id
        AND ps.is_local = 1 /* Primary */
       WHERE drs.is_local = 0 /* Secondary */
         AND DATEDIFF(SECOND,ISNULL (drs.last_commit_time, drs.Last_hardened_time), ps.last_commit_time) > 60
  ) 
        THEN 1
     ELSE 0
    END
RecommendedAction: Resolve the finding for AG Replica Falling Behind. See technical_details for exact remediation steps and verification queries.
ExpectedResult: Returns PASS when no instances of 'AG Replica Falling Behind' are detected. Returns FAIL or WARN when the condition exists and requires attention.
BusinessTranslation: Downtime during business hours costs revenue per minute. Every unmitigated availability gap is a bet against hardware that will eventually fail.
